title: Crispy Malaysian Tofu Soup
description: Fresh, crisp, monsoon Malaysian dish featuring a vegetable stock cube, simmered to perfection and high-protein-friendly.
instructions: Heat a wok or deep pan over medium heat and add 1 tsp sesame oil.
Add the diced tofu and fry until all sides are golden and crisp, then remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add minced ginger and garlic, stir until fragrant (about 30s).
Add sliced carrots and shiitake mushrooms, cook for 2 min until slightly softened.
Pour in the dissolved vegetable stock, bring to a gentle boil.
Stir in 1 tbsp soy sauce and 1 tsp lime juice, season with salt and pepper; reduce heat and simmer for 5 min.
Add bean sprouts and chopped green onions, simmer for another 2 min until sprouts just wilt.
Return the fried tofu to the pan, heat through for 1 min, then remove from heat.
Serve hot in bowls, garnishing with extra green onions if desired.
Enjoy with a side of steamed rice or a light salad if desired.
